commands.name: materials-init
commands.description: Initialize a new materials research project with Zotero and Obsidian integration
commands.prompt:
Initialize a new materials research project. Ask the user for:
1. Project name (e.g., 'CO2-hydrogenation-catalyst')
2. Research focus description
3. Principal investigator name (optional)
4. Whether to create Zotero collections (default: yes)

Then execute the materials-init skill with these parameters.

commands.name: characterization
commands.description: Analyze characterization data (XRD, BET, TEM, XPS, electrochemistry)
commands.prompt:
Analyze characterization data. Ask the user for:
1. Data type (XRD/BET/TEM/XPS/Electrochemistry)
2. File path or data input
3. Material ID
4. Save location in project

Then execute the characterization-analysis skill.

commands.name: catalysis-results
commands.description: Evaluate catalysis performance (conversion, selectivity, TOF, stability)
commands.prompt:
Evaluate catalysis performance. Ask the user for:
1. Experimental data file or manual input
2. Material ID
3. Reaction conditions
4. Analysis type (activity/selectivity/stability/full)

Then execute the catalysis-performance skill.

commands.name: dft-analysis
commands.description: Parse and analyze DFT computational results
commands.prompt:
Analyze DFT computational results. Ask the user for:
1. Output file path (Gaussian .log, ORCA .out, VASP OUTCAR)
2. Analysis type (energy/geometry/frequency/mechanism)
3. Material or reaction system

Then execute the computational-workflow skill.

commands.name: synthesis-log
commands.description: Document synthesis procedures and batch records
commands.prompt:
Document synthesis procedure. Ask the user for:
1. Material name/ID
2. Synthesis method
3. Reagents and conditions
4. Batch number

Then execute the synthesis-protocol skill.

commands.name: manuscript-draft
commands.description: Generate manuscript draft from Obsidian research notes and experimental data
commands.prompt:
Generate a manuscript draft by compiling data from the Research project directory.

Ask the user for:
1. Project name (e.g., 'CO2-hydrogenation-catalyst')
2. Target journal (ACS Catalysis, Journal of Catalysis, Nature Materials, etc.)
3. Manuscript title
4. Key message / main finding

Then:
- Scan Research/{project}/Characterization/ for characterization data
- Scan Research/{project}/Reactions/Results/ for performance data
- Scan Research/{project}/Computational/ for DFT results
- Scan Research/{project}/Papers/ for literature citations
- Generate IMRAD-structured manuscript with:
  * Abstract (quantitative results)
  * Introduction (context, gap, objective)
  * Experimental (synthesis, characterization methods, catalytic tests)
  * Results and Discussion (characterization, performance, mechanism)
  * Conclusions
  * References (from Zotero/Papers notes)
- Save to Research/{project}/Writing/manuscript_draft.md

commands.name: paper-review
commands.description: Review manuscript for completeness and quality
commands.prompt:
Review manuscript. Ask the user for:
1. Manuscript file path
2. Target journal
3. Review focus (structure/data/citations/figures)

Then execute the materials-paper-review skill.
